question: According to the prevalence categories shown on the map, which statement is most accurate?

options.A: Region J has a higher prevalence level than Region G.
options.B: Region G has a higher prevalence level than Region J.
options.C: Region J and Region G share the same prevalence level.
options.D: Neither Region J nor Region G has a reported prevalence level.

explanation: The color legend is ordered in a way that does not reflect the true ranking of prevalence levels, and the colors themselves are not in an intuitive Low→High sequence. From the underlying data, Region J is classified at a higher level than Region G, even though the legend ordering may make the opposite appear plausible at first glance.
